prompts.title: Write a Business Plan Executive Summary
prompts.description: Generate a clear and professional executive summary for a business plan.
stages: What is your business name and what does it do?
Who is your target customer?
What problem does your business solve?
What makes your business different from competitors?
What are your main revenue streams?

prompts.title: Create a Social Media Content Calendar
prompts.description: Plan a month of social media posts tailored to your business goals.
stages: What platforms are you posting on?
What is your business niche?
How many posts per week do you want?
What are your content goals (sales, awareness, engagement)?
Do you have any upcoming promotions or events?

prompts.title: Draft a Professional Business Email
prompts.description: Write a polished email for client communication, proposals, or follow-ups.
stages: Who are you emailing and what is their role?
What is the main purpose of this email?
What tone do you want (formal, friendly, urgent)?
What action do you want the reader to take?
Any key details or context to include?

prompts.title: Write a Product or Service Description
prompts.description: Create a compelling description that highlights value and drives sales.
stages: What is the product or service name?
What does it do and who is it for?
What are its top 3 benefits?
What problem does it solve for the customer?
What is the price range or offer?

prompts.title: Generate Customer FAQ Responses
prompts.description: Build clear and helpful FAQ answers for your business website or support team.
stages: What is your business type?
What are the top 5 questions your customers ask?
What tone should the answers use?
Are there policies or guarantees to mention?
How long should each answer be?

prompts.title: Create a Customer Onboarding Message
prompts.description: Write a warm, clear welcome message for new customers or clients.
stages: What product or service did the customer just purchase?
What should the customer do first after joining?
What resources or links do you want to share?
What tone fits your brand (warm, professional, casual)?
Is there a support contact or community to mention?

prompts.title: Write a Sales Script for Phone or DM Outreach
prompts.description: Create a natural and persuasive script for reaching out to potential clients.
stages: Who are you reaching out to and why?
What product or service are you pitching?
What pain point does it solve for them?
What is your ask or call to action?
What objections might they raise?

prompts.title: Draft a Business Partnership Proposal
prompts.description: Write a professional proposal to approach a potential business partner or collaborator.
stages: Who are you proposing the partnership to?
What type of partnership are you proposing?
What value does each party bring?
What are the expected outcomes?
What is the next step you are proposing?

prompts.title: Create a Weekly Business Performance Report
prompts.description: Summarize your business results, wins, and areas to improve in a clear format.
stages: What metrics do you track weekly (sales, leads, revenue, etc.)?
What were the results this week?
What went well?
What did not go as planned?
What is the priority focus for next week?

prompts.title: Write a Refund or Complaint Response
prompts.description: Handle difficult customer situations with a calm and professional reply.
stages: What was the customer's complaint or request?
What is your refund or resolution policy?
Do you want to offer a replacement, refund, or apology?
What tone do you want (empathetic, firm, solution-focused)?
Is there anything to avoid saying?

prompts.title: Build a Competitive Analysis Summary
prompts.description: Compare your business to 2 to 3 competitors and identify your advantages.
stages: What is your business and what do you offer?
Who are your 2 to 3 main competitors?
What do they do well?
Where do they fall short?
What is your unique competitive advantage?

prompts.title: Write a Launch Announcement Post
prompts.description: Create a compelling post to announce a new product, service, or business milestone.
stages: What are you launching?
What makes it valuable to your audience?
What platform is this for (LinkedIn, Instagram, email, etc.)?
Is there an offer, discount, or deadline attached?
What action should readers take after reading?

prompts.title: Create a 90-Day Business Growth Plan
prompts.description: Build a focused action plan to grow your business over the next 3 months.
stages: What is your main business goal for the next 90 days?
What are your current biggest bottlenecks?
What resources do you have (budget, team, tools)?
What strategies do you want to focus on (marketing, sales, operations)?
How will you measure success at the end of 90 days?

prompts.title: Write a Pricing Justification Message
prompts.description: Explain your pricing confidently to customers who push back or question your rates.
stages: What is your product or service and its price?
What key value does it deliver to the client?
What objection are you responding to?
What makes your offer worth the price?
What tone should this message use?

prompts.title: Create a Business SWOT Analysis
prompts.description: Identify your strengths, weaknesses, opportunities, and threats in a structured format.
stages: What type of business do you run?
What do you consider your biggest strengths right now?
What are your current weaknesses or gaps?
What opportunities do you see in your market?
What external threats or risks concern you?

prompts.title: Summarize a Long Article or Textbook Chapter
prompts.description: Get a clear, concise summary of any long piece of academic content.
stages: What subject is this content from?
What is the main topic or chapter title?
Paste the text or describe its key sections.
How long should the summary be?
Do you need key terms or definitions highlighted?

prompts.title: Explain a Difficult Concept in Simple Terms
prompts.description: Break down a complex topic so it is easy to understand.
stages: What concept do you want explained?
What subject or course is it from?
What do you already understand about it?
What part is confusing you most?
Do you want an analogy or real-life example?

prompts.title: Create a Study Schedule
prompts.description: Build a realistic study plan based on your exams, deadlines, and available time.
stages: What subjects or courses do you need to study?
When are your exams or deadlines?
How many hours per day can you study?
What time of day do you study best?
Do you prefer one subject per day or mixed sessions?

prompts.title: Write a Strong Essay Introduction
prompts.description: Generate a compelling opening paragraph for any academic essay.
stages: What is your essay topic?
What is your main argument or thesis?
What subject or course is this for?
What academic level is this (high school, undergraduate, postgraduate)?
Should it start with a hook, question, or fact?

prompts.title: Generate Practice Questions for an Exam
prompts.description: Create realistic practice questions to test your understanding before an exam.
stages: What subject and topic are you preparing for?
What type of questions do you need (multiple choice, essay, short answer)?
How many questions do you want?
What difficulty level (easy, medium, hard)?
Should answers be included?

prompts.title: Proofread and Improve an Essay or Assignment
prompts.description: Get feedback on grammar, structure, clarity, and academic tone.
stages: What is the essay topic or assignment question?
What academic level and subject is this?
Paste your essay or a section of it.
What specific areas do you want feedback on?
Should the tone be kept formal or academic?

prompts.title: Create Flashcards for a Topic
prompts.description: Turn your notes or a topic into ready-to-use flashcard pairs.
stages: What topic or subject are the flashcards for?
How many flashcards do you need?
Should they be term-and-definition or question-and-answer format?
What level of detail is needed?
Any specific subtopics to focus on?

prompts.title: Write a Research Paper Outline
prompts.description: Build a structured outline for a research paper on any topic.
stages: What is your research topic?
What is your research question or thesis?
How long is the paper (pages or word count)?
What sections are required (introduction, literature review, methodology, etc.)?
What citation style do you use (APA, MLA, Harvard)?

prompts.title: Understand and Solve a Math Problem Step by Step
prompts.description: Get a clear walkthrough of how to solve a math problem with explanations.
stages: What is the math topic (algebra, calculus, statistics, etc.)?
Type or describe the problem you need help with.
What step are you stuck on?
Do you want the formula explained before the solution?
Should the answer be shown at the end or revealed step by step?

prompts.title: Prepare for a Class Presentation or Defence
prompts.description: Get help structuring your presentation and anticipating questions.
stages: What is your presentation topic?
How long is the presentation?
Who is your audience (classmates, lecturers, panel)?
What key points must you cover?
What questions might you be asked?

prompts.title: Write a Cover Letter for an Internship
prompts.description: Craft a professional cover letter for a student internship application.
stages: What internship role are you applying for?
What company or organisation is it with?
What are your key academic or extracurricular strengths?
Why do you want this specific internship?
What skills or experience make you a good fit?

prompts.title: Analyse a Case Study
prompts.description: Break down a case study and produce a structured academic analysis.
stages: What subject or course is this case study for?
Briefly describe the case or paste key details.
What specific questions must your analysis answer?
What frameworks should you apply (SWOT, PESTLE, Porter's Five Forces, etc.)?
How long should the analysis be?

prompts.title: Build a Personal Study Note System
prompts.description: Design a note-taking structure that fits your learning style and subject load.
stages: How many subjects are you currently taking?
What note-taking methods have you tried before?
Do you prefer digital or paper notes?
What is your biggest struggle with note-taking?
When do you usually review your notes?

prompts.title: Write a Literature Review Section
prompts.description: Synthesise academic sources into a well-structured literature review.
stages: What is your research topic or thesis?
List the key sources or authors you are reviewing.
What themes or arguments are you comparing?
How many words is this section?
What citation style is required?

prompts.title: Turn Lecture Notes into a Full Study Guide
prompts.description: Convert raw lecture notes into an organised, easy-to-revise study guide.
stages: What subject and topic do these notes cover?
Paste your raw notes or describe the key points.
Should key terms be defined separately?
Do you want diagrams or tables suggested?
How long should the study guide be?

prompts.title: Write an Instagram Caption with Hashtags
prompts.description: Create an engaging caption for any Instagram post with relevant hashtags.
stages: What is the post about?
What is the mood or tone (motivational, funny, informative, personal)?
Who is your target audience?
Should it include a call to action?
How many hashtags do you want and what niche are they for?

prompts.title: Write a Twitter or X Thread
prompts.description: Turn an idea or topic into a compelling multi-tweet thread that holds attention.
stages: What is the main topic or lesson for the thread?
How many tweets do you want in the thread?
What is the hook or opening tweet?
What key points should each tweet cover?
How should the thread end (call to action, summary, question)?

prompts.title: Write a LinkedIn Post
prompts.description: Craft a professional LinkedIn post that builds your personal brand and gets engagement.
stages: What topic or story do you want to share?
What is the main takeaway or lesson?
What is your professional niche or industry?
Should it include a personal experience or data point?
What do you want readers to do after reading?

prompts.title: Write a TikTok Video Script
prompts.description: Create a short, punchy script for a TikTok video that grabs attention fast.
stages: What is the video topic or idea?
How long is the video (15, 30, or 60 seconds)?
What is the hook in the first 3 seconds?
What tone do you want (funny, educational, inspirational)?
Should there be a call to action at the end?

prompts.title: Write Replies to Comments or DMs
prompts.description: Generate friendly, on-brand responses to audience comments and messages.
stages: What platform are the comments on?
What is the general type of comment (question, compliment, criticism)?
What is your content niche?
What tone matches your brand (casual, professional, witty)?
Paste 1 to 3 sample comments you want to respond to.

prompts.title: Write a Blog Post Outline
prompts.description: Structure a clear, SEO-friendly blog post outline on any topic.
stages: What is the blog post topic?
Who is your target reader?
How long should the post be (word count)?
What main sections should be covered?
Should it include an intro, FAQs, and a conclusion?

prompts.title: Write a YouTube Video Script
prompts.description: Create a full script for a YouTube video including intro, body, and outro.
stages: What is the video topic?
How long should the video be (minutes)?
Who is the target audience?
What key points or sections should the script cover?
Should it include a subscribe reminder or sponsor section?

prompts.title: Write a Newsletter Edition
prompts.description: Create a complete newsletter issue for your subscribers.
stages: What is the newsletter niche or theme?
What is this edition about?
What sections does your newsletter usually include?
What tone do you use with your audience?
Is there a product, link, or offer to mention?

prompts.title: Write a Long-Form Blog Article
prompts.description: Create a full, detailed blog article optimised for readers and search engines.
stages: What is the article topic and target keyword?
Who is the reader and what do they need to know?
What is the word count target?
What structure should it follow?
Should it include examples, stats, or expert quotes?

prompts.title: Create 10 Viral Hook Ideas
prompts.description: Generate strong opening lines for posts, videos, or articles that stop the scroll.
stages: What topic or niche are the hooks for?
What platform are they for (Instagram, TikTok, Twitter, blog)?
What emotion should the hook trigger (curiosity, surprise, urgency, fear)?
Who is your target audience?
Should they be questions, statements, or bold claims?

prompts.title: Repurpose One Piece of Content into Multiple Formats
prompts.description: Turn a blog post, video, or article into content for multiple platforms.
stages: What is the original content (paste or describe it)?
What formats do you want it repurposed into?
Which platforms will each format be posted on?
Should the tone stay the same or adapt per platform?
Are there specific highlights or quotes to prioritise?

prompts.title: Build a 30-Day Content Calendar
prompts.description: Plan a full month of content ideas across your key platforms.
stages: What platforms are you posting on?
What is your niche or content focus?
How many times per week do you post on each platform?
Do you have any campaigns, launches, or events this month?
What content pillars or themes do you rotate through?

prompts.title: Write Stronger Call-to-Action Lines
prompts.description: Craft compelling CTAs that push your audience to click, follow, buy, or share.
stages: What action do you want your audience to take?
What platform or content type is this for?
What is your niche or offer?
Should the CTA feel urgent, soft, or conversational?
How many CTA options do you want?

prompts.title: Grow Your Audience Strategy Plan
prompts.description: Build a practical strategy to grow followers and reach on your chosen platform.
stages: What platform are you trying to grow on?
What is your current follower count and niche?
What content formats are you currently using?
What has worked or failed so far?
What is your growth goal in the next 90 days?

prompts.title: Analyse Content Performance and Suggest Improvements
prompts.description: Review your content results and get specific actions to improve performance.
stages: What platform and content type are you reviewing?
What were your top-performing and worst-performing posts?
What metrics matter most to you (reach, likes, saves, clicks)?
What patterns do you notice in the data?
What content goals are you not hitting yet?

prompts.title: Write a Facebook or Instagram Ad
prompts.description: Create a scroll-stopping ad copy with headline, body, and call to action.
stages: What product or service are you advertising?
Who is the target audience?
What is the main benefit or hook?
What action should the ad drive (buy, sign up, learn more)?
What is the budget context (cold audience or retargeting)?

prompts.title: Write a Google Search Ad
prompts.description: Generate keyword-focused Google ad copy with headlines and descriptions.
stages: What is the product or service being advertised?
What are the top 2 to 3 keywords you are targeting?
What makes your offer stand out from competitors?
What is the landing page destination?
What call to action do you want?

prompts.title: Write a Cold Email Campaign Sequence
prompts.description: Build a 3 to 5 email sequence for cold outreach that generates replies.
stages: Who are you emailing and what do they do?
What product or service are you pitching?
What specific pain point does it address?
How many emails in the sequence?
What tone and approach should the sequence use?

prompts.title: Map Out a Sales Funnel
prompts.description: Design a simple funnel from awareness to conversion for any offer.
stages: What is the product or service you are selling?
Who is your ideal customer?
What is the price point?
What traffic source are you using (ads, organic, email)?
What conversion action is the funnel built around?

prompts.title: Write a Retargeting Ad Script
prompts.description: Create ad copy aimed at people who have already visited your site or engaged with your content.
stages: What did the audience do (visited site, added to cart, watched video)?
What is the offer or product?
What objection likely stopped them from converting?
What urgency or incentive can you offer?
Which platform is this retargeting ad for?

prompts.title: Write a Landing Page
prompts.description: Create a full landing page with headline, subheadline, benefits, and CTA.
stages: What product, service, or lead magnet is this page for?
Who is the target visitor?
What is the single goal of this page?
What are the top 3 to 5 benefits to highlight?
What objections should the page address?

prompts.title: Write Product Descriptions That Sell
prompts.description: Create persuasive product descriptions that highlight benefits and push buyers to act.
stages: What is the product name and category?
What are its key features?
What benefits does it give the buyer?
Who is the ideal buyer?
What tone fits the brand (premium, playful, professional)?

prompts.title: Write 10 Headline Variations
prompts.description: Generate multiple headline options for ads, landing pages, or articles.
stages: What is the product, service, or content the headline is for?
Who is the target audience?
What main benefit or hook should be included?
What tone should the headlines use?
Should they include a number, question, or power word?

prompts.title: Define a Brand Voice and Messaging Guide
prompts.description: Create a short brand voice guide that keeps your copy consistent across all channels.
stages: What type of business or brand is this for?
Who is your target audience?
What 3 to 5 words describe how you want your brand to sound?
What tone should be avoided?
Can you share an example of copy you like or already use?

prompts.title: Write Copy That Handles Objections
prompts.description: Create sales copy that addresses and overcomes common buyer hesitations.
stages: What product or service are you selling?
What are the top 3 objections buyers raise?
What evidence or reassurance do you have to counter each objection?
Where will this copy appear (sales page, email, ad)?
What tone should it use (confident, empathetic, direct)?

prompts.title: Write a Value Proposition Statement
prompts.description: Craft a one to two sentence statement that clearly explains what you do and why it matters.
stages: What does your business do?
Who do you serve?
What specific result or benefit do you deliver?
What makes you different from alternatives?
What format do you need (tagline, full statement, elevator pitch)?

prompts.title: Build a Customer Avatar Profile
prompts.description: Create a detailed profile of your ideal customer to sharpen your targeting and messaging.
stages: What product or service are you marketing?
What do you already know about your existing customers?
What demographics matter most (age, location, income, job)?
What are their main goals and frustrations?
Where do they spend time online?

prompts.title: Write a 90-Day Content Marketing Plan
prompts.description: Build a strategic content plan to attract, nurture, and convert your target audience.
stages: What is the business goal this content plan supports?
Who is the target audience?
What content formats will you use (blog, video, email, social)?
What topics or pillars will you cover?
How will you measure content performance?

prompts.title: Design an A/B Test Plan for an Ad or Email
prompts.description: Set up a simple A/B test to find what copy, subject line, or creative performs best.
stages: What are you testing (ad, email, landing page)?
What specific element are you testing (headline, image, CTA, subject line)?
What are the two variations you want to test?
What is the success metric (open rate, click rate, conversion rate)?
How long will the test run and what sample size do you have?

prompts.title: Review and Improve Existing Marketing Copy
prompts.description: Analyse your current copy and get specific edits to make it stronger.
stages: Paste the copy you want reviewed.
What is this copy for (ad, email, landing page, product description)?
What result is it currently getting?
What do you think is weak about it?
What should improved copy focus on achieving?

prompts.title: Generate a Logo Concept Brief
prompts.description: Create a detailed brief to guide a logo design for any brand or business.
stages: What is the brand name and what does it do?
Who is the target audience?
What feeling should the logo communicate (bold, elegant, playful, minimal)?
Are there any colours or styles to include or avoid?
What formats will the logo be used in (digital, print, apparel)?

prompts.title: Build a Brand Identity Guide
prompts.description: Create a simple brand identity document covering colours, fonts, tone, and logo usage.
stages: What is the brand name and business type?
What are the brand values or personality traits?
What colour palette do you currently use or want?
What fonts do you use or prefer?
Who is the primary audience for this brand?

prompts.title: Suggest a UI Colour Palette
prompts.description: Generate a balanced, accessible colour palette for a website or app interface.
stages: What is the app or website for?
What mood should the design convey (calm, energetic, professional, friendly)?
Is there an existing brand colour to build around?
Who is the end user?
Should the palette meet accessibility contrast standards?

prompts.title: Write a Typography Pairing Recommendation
prompts.description: Get font pairing suggestions for a website, brand, or design project.
stages: What is the design project (website, app, poster, brand)?
What mood or style should the typography reflect?
Should fonts be from Google Fonts, Adobe Fonts, or system fonts?
Do you need a heading font, body font, or both?
Is there an existing font you are building around?

prompts.title: Write a UX Copy for App Screens
prompts.description: Create clear, user-friendly copy for buttons, tooltips, error messages, and onboarding screens.
stages: What type of app is this (productivity, e-commerce, finance, health)?
Which screens need copy (onboarding, error states, empty states, buttons)?
What tone should the copy use (friendly, professional, simple)?
Who is the end user?
Are there any words or phrases the brand avoids?

prompts.title: Design a Social Media Graphic Brief
prompts.description: Write a detailed visual brief for a designer or AI tool to create a social media graphic.
stages: What platform is the graphic for (Instagram, LinkedIn, Facebook, Twitter)?
What is the purpose of the post (promotion, announcement, quote, event)?
What text or headline should appear on the graphic?
What colours, fonts, or brand elements should be used?
What mood or visual style should it have?

prompts.title: Write a YouTube Thumbnail Concept
prompts.description: Generate a thumbnail idea with layout, text, and visual direction that drives clicks.
stages: What is the video topic?
What emotion should the thumbnail trigger (curiosity, urgency, excitement)?
Should a face or reaction appear in the thumbnail?
What short text or number should appear on it?
What are the brand colours or style guidelines?

prompts.title: Plan a Presentation Slide Deck Layout
prompts.description: Get a structured slide-by-slide layout plan for any presentation.
stages: What is the presentation topic?
Who is the audience?
How many slides do you need?
What key message must the deck communicate?
What visual style fits the context (corporate, creative, minimal)?

prompts.title: Write a Marketing Visual Brief
prompts.description: Create a clear brief for a designer to produce a marketing banner, flyer, or poster.
stages: What is the marketing material (banner, flyer, poster, email header)?
What is the campaign or message?
What are the dimensions or format requirements?
What brand assets must be included?
What is the call to action or key text?

prompts.title: Get Feedback on a Design Concept
prompts.description: Analyse your design idea and receive constructive critique on clarity, hierarchy, and impact.
stages: Describe your design concept or paste a description of it.
What is the design for and who is the audience?
What problem is the design trying to solve?
What do you feel is working well so far?
What specific areas do you want feedback on?

prompts.title: Write a Client Design Brief Template
prompts.description: Create a professional design brief template to collect clear project requirements from clients.
stages: What type of design projects do you typically take on?
What information do you always need from clients before starting?
What questions help you understand their brand and audience?
How formal or conversational should the brief feel?
Should it include a section for budget, timeline, and revision limits?

prompts.title: Create a Design System Starter Kit
prompts.description: Build the foundation of a design system with components, tokens, and usage rules.
stages: What product or platform is this design system for?
What components do you need to define first (buttons, inputs, cards, typography)?
What design tokens need to be established (spacing, colour, radius, shadow)?
What team will use this system (designers, developers, or both)?
Are there existing brand guidelines to align with?

prompts.title: Audit and Improve a Website Visual Design
prompts.description: Review your current website design and get specific improvement recommendations.
stages: What is the website URL or describe its current design?
What is the site's purpose and main audience?
What visual issues or complaints have you noticed?
What parts of the design do you want to keep?
What is the priority area for improvement (hero, navigation, CTA, mobile layout)?

prompts.title: Plan a Brand Refresh Strategy
prompts.description: Build a step-by-step plan to update an existing brand without losing recognition.
stages: What brand are you refreshing and how old is it?
What currently works well that should stay?
What feels outdated or misaligned with your audience?
What new direction or feeling should the brand move toward?
What assets need to be updated (logo, colours, website, packaging)?

prompts.title: Write a Design Trend Analysis for Your Niche
prompts.description: Get a breakdown of current design trends relevant to your industry and how to apply them.
stages: What industry or niche is this design trend analysis for?
What design formats are most relevant to you (web, social, packaging, print)?
What has your current design style looked like?
Are you looking to follow trends or differentiate from them?
What is the goal of updating your visual approach?

prompts.title: Write a Function to Solve a Specific Task
prompts.description: Generate a clean, working function in your chosen language to handle a specific coding task.
stages: What programming language are you using?
What should the function do?
What inputs will it receive?
What should it return or output?
Are there any edge cases or constraints to handle?

prompts.title: Build a Simple Script to Automate a Task
prompts.description: Create a script that automates a repetitive task such as file renaming, data formatting, or sending requests.
stages: What task do you want to automate?
What language should the script be written in?
What inputs or files does the script work with?
What should happen when the script runs?
Should the script log results or handle errors?

prompts.title: Generate a REST API Endpoint
prompts.description: Write a working REST API endpoint for a specific action such as creating, reading, updating, or deleting data.
stages: What framework or language is the API built with?
What does this endpoint need to do (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE)?
What data does it receive and what does it return?
Should it include validation or error handling?
Is there any authentication or middleware to consider?

prompts.title: Create a Reusable UI Component
prompts.description: Build a clean, reusable front-end component such as a button, modal, card, or form input.
stages: What framework are you using (React, Vue, plain HTML/CSS)?
What component do you need (button, modal, form, card, dropdown)?
What props or options should it accept?
Should it include any interaction logic or state?
Are there style guidelines or a design system to follow?

prompts.title: Write a Database Query for a Specific Use Case
prompts.description: Generate a SQL or NoSQL query to fetch, filter, or update data based on your use case.
stages: What database are you using (PostgreSQL, MySQL, MongoDB, SQLite)?
What data do you need to retrieve or change?
What tables or collections are involved?
Are there filters, joins, or conditions required?
Should the query be optimized for performance?

prompts.title: Debug an Error in My Code
prompts.description: Identify the cause of a bug or error message and get a clear fix with explanation.
stages: What language or framework is the code written in?
Paste the code that is causing the error.
What error message or unexpected behaviour are you seeing?
What did you expect the code to do?
What have you already tried to fix it?

prompts.title: Review My Code for Quality and Best Practices
prompts.description: Get a structured code review covering readability, structure, naming, and potential issues.
stages: What language is this code written in?
Paste the code you want reviewed.
What is the code supposed to do?
Are there specific areas you are worried about?
Should the review focus on performance, readability, or both?

prompts.title: Optimise Slow or Inefficient Code
prompts.description: Analyse code that is slow, bloated, or repetitive and rewrite it to run more efficiently.
stages: What language or environment is the code running in?
Paste the code that needs optimisation.
What performance issue are you experiencing (slow load, high memory, slow queries)?
Is there a specific bottleneck you have already identified?
Are there any constraints such as library restrictions or runtime limits?

prompts.title: Convert Code from One Language to Another
prompts.description: Translate a working code snippet or function from one programming language to another.
stages: What language is the original code written in?
Paste the code you want to convert.
What language should it be converted to?
Should the logic stay exactly the same or can it use idiomatic patterns?
Are there any libraries or dependencies to account for?

prompts.title: Write Unit Tests for My Code
prompts.description: Generate unit tests to cover the key behaviours and edge cases of a function or module.
stages: What language or testing framework should be used (Jest, PyTest, JUnit, etc.)?
Paste the function or module to be tested.
What are the expected inputs and outputs?
Are there edge cases or error states that must be tested?
Should mocks or stubs be used for external dependencies?

prompts.title: Explain How a Piece of Code Works
prompts.description: Break down any code snippet line by line so you understand exactly what it does and why.
stages: Paste the code you want explained.
What language is it written in?
What is your current level of coding experience?
Is there a specific part you are most confused about?
Should the explanation be simple and plain or more technical?

prompts.title: Learn a Programming Concept with Examples
prompts.description: Get a clear explanation of any coding concept with simple examples you can run and test.
stages: What concept do you want to learn (loops, recursion, APIs, async, etc.)?
What language should the examples use?
How familiar are you with coding in general?
Do you want a real-world analogy to help explain it?
Should the explanation include a short practice exercise?

prompts.title: Build a Learning Roadmap for a Programming Goal
prompts.description: Get a step-by-step learning plan to reach a specific coding goal such as building apps, getting a job, or learning a new language.
stages: What is your coding goal (get a job, build a product, learn a framework)?
What is your current skill level?
How many hours per week can you commit to learning?
What language or technology area are you focused on?
Do you prefer hands-on projects or structured theory first?

prompts.title: Design the Architecture for a New App or System
prompts.description: Plan the technical structure of a new application including components, data flow, and technology choices.
stages: What type of app or system are you building?
Who are the end users and how many do you expect?
What are the core features the system must support?
Do you have preferred languages, frameworks, or cloud providers?
What are the biggest technical risks or constraints?

prompts.title: Refactor a Codebase for Scalability
prompts.description: Restructure existing code to improve maintainability, scalability, and team readability.
stages: What language and framework is the codebase using?
What is the main problem with the current structure (hard to maintain, too slow, messy)?
Paste a representative sample of the code or describe its structure.
How large is the codebase and how many people work on it?
Are there specific patterns you want to move toward (MVC, microservices, clean architecture)?

prompts.title: Write a Lesson Plan for Any Subject
prompts.description: Generate a structured lesson plan with objectives, activities, and assessment for any topic or grade level.
stages: What subject and topic is the lesson covering?
What grade level or age group is this for?
How long is the lesson (30, 45, or 60 minutes)?
What should students be able to do by the end of the lesson?
Are there any resources, tools, or constraints to consider?

prompts.title: Create a Quiz or Assessment for a Topic
prompts.description: Build a ready-to-use quiz with questions, answer types, and an answer key for any subject.
stages: What subject and topic should the quiz cover?
What grade level is this for?
How many questions do you need?
What question types should be included (multiple choice, short answer, true or false)?
What difficulty level should the questions target?

prompts.title: Design a Grading Rubric
prompts.description: Create a clear, fair rubric with criteria and scoring levels for any assignment or project.
stages: What assignment or task is being graded?
What grade level or course is this for?
What are the key skills or outcomes being assessed?
How many performance levels should the rubric have (3, 4, or 5)?
Should the rubric use points, percentages, or descriptive labels?

prompts.title: Write Clear Learning Objectives
prompts.description: Turn a broad topic into specific, measurable learning objectives students can understand and work toward.
stages: What subject and topic are the objectives for?
What grade level or age group is this targeting?
How many objectives do you need?
What cognitive level should they target (recall, apply, analyse, create)?
Should objectives follow a specific format such as Bloom's Taxonomy?

prompts.title: Build a Unit Plan for a Topic
prompts.description: Plan a multi-week unit with lessons, assessments, and key milestones for a specific topic.
stages: What subject and topic does the unit cover?
What grade level is this for?
How many weeks or lessons should the unit span?
What are the key outcomes students must reach by the end?
Are there any standards or curriculum frameworks to align with?

prompts.title: Generate Discussion Questions for a Text or Topic
prompts.description: Create thought-provoking questions that spark classroom discussion and deeper thinking.
stages: What text, topic, or concept is being discussed?
What grade level or age group is this for?
How many questions do you need?
Should questions be open-ended, text-based, or opinion-driven?
What thinking skills should the questions develop (analysis, reflection, debate)?

prompts.title: Design an Engaging Classroom Activity
prompts.description: Create a hands-on or interactive activity that helps students practise or explore a concept.
stages: What concept or skill should the activity teach or reinforce?
What grade level or age group is it for?
How much time is available for the activity?
Should it be individual, paired, or group work?
Are there any materials or technology limitations?

prompts.title: Differentiate a Lesson for Mixed Ability Groups
prompts.description: Adapt a lesson or activity to meet the needs of students with different skill levels in the same class.
stages: What is the original lesson or activity?
What subject and grade level is this for?
What ability levels are you differentiating for (struggling, on level, advanced)?
What specific challenges do your lower or higher level students face?
Should the differentiation change the task, the support, or the expected output?

prompts.title: Create a Project-Based Learning Task
prompts.description: Design a real-world project that connects curriculum goals to a meaningful student challenge.
stages: What subject and topic should the project connect to?
What grade level is this for?
How long should the project run (1 week, 2 weeks, longer)?
What real-world problem or scenario should students engage with?
How will the project be assessed or presented?

prompts.title: Gamify a Lesson or Unit
prompts.description: Add game mechanics such as points, challenges, levels, or rewards to make learning more engaging.
stages: What lesson, unit, or subject do you want to gamify?
What grade level or age group is this for?
What game elements appeal to your students (points, leaderboards, badges, challenges)?
How much class time can be spent on the gamified element?
Should it be competitive, collaborative, or individual?

prompts.title: Write a Parent Communication Email
prompts.description: Draft a clear, professional email to a parent about student progress, behaviour, or an upcoming event.
stages: What is the purpose of the email (progress update, concern, event, positive news)?
What grade level or subject do you teach?
What key information needs to be communicated?
What tone should the email use (warm, direct, formal)?
Is there any action you need the parent to take?

prompts.title: Write Meaningful Student Feedback
prompts.description: Generate specific, constructive feedback on student work that guides improvement without discouraging effort.
stages: What assignment or task is the feedback for?
What did the student do well?
What are the main areas that need improvement?
What is the student's current level (struggling, meeting expectations, exceeding)?
Should the feedback be written for the student directly or for a report?

prompts.title: Create a Classroom Behaviour Management Plan
prompts.description: Build a practical plan with clear expectations, consequences, and positive strategies for managing classroom behaviour.
stages: What age group or grade level is the plan for?
What are the main behaviour challenges in your classroom?
What positive reinforcement strategies do you want to use?
What consequence structure should be in place?
Should the plan include communication steps with parents or leadership?

prompts.title: Write a Student Progress Report
prompts.description: Generate a clear, balanced progress report for a student covering academic performance and effort.
stages: What subject or subjects does the report cover?
What is the student's current level of performance?
What are their strengths?
What areas need development?
What tone should the report use and who is the audience (parents, school admin)?

prompts.title: Design a Curriculum Scope and Sequence
prompts.description: Map out what to teach, in what order, and when across a full term or year for a subject.
stages: What subject are you mapping the curriculum for?
What grade level or course is this for?
How long is the teaching period (one term, two terms, full year)?
What are the key topics or units that must be covered?
Are there curriculum standards or frameworks to align with?

prompts.title: Write or Improve My CV
prompts.description: Create a polished, role-specific CV that highlights your experience, skills, and achievements clearly.
stages: What role or industry are you targeting?
What is your current job title and years of experience?
List your last 2 to 3 roles with responsibilities and key achievements.
What skills, tools, or certifications are relevant to the role?
Should the CV follow a specific format or length requirement?

prompts.title: Write a Cover Letter for a Job Application
prompts.description: Draft a focused cover letter that connects your experience to a specific job and company.
stages: What is the job title and company you are applying to?
What are the key requirements listed in the job description?
What relevant experience or achievements make you a strong fit?
Why do you want to work at this specific company?
What tone should the letter use (formal, confident, conversational)?

prompts.title: Optimise My LinkedIn Profile
prompts.description: Rewrite your LinkedIn headline, summary, and experience sections to attract recruiters and opportunities.
stages: What is your current job title and field?
What type of roles or opportunities are you looking for?
What are your top 3 professional strengths or achievements?
What keywords are common in your target job descriptions?
Should the tone be professional, approachable, or a mix of both?

prompts.title: Build a Job Search Strategy
prompts.description: Create a focused plan for finding and landing a job in your target field within a specific timeframe.
stages: What type of role are you looking for?
What is your current employment situation?
What is your target timeframe to land a new role?
What platforms or methods have you already tried?
Are there location, remote, or industry preferences to factor in?

prompts.title: Write a Job Application Follow-Up Email
prompts.description: Draft a professional follow-up email after submitting an application or completing an interview.
stages: What stage are you following up on (application, interview, offer)?
What is the job title and company name?
How long ago did you submit or interview?
What is the main goal of the email (check status, express interest, ask for feedback)?
What tone should the email use?

prompts.title: Prepare for a Job Interview
prompts.description: Get a tailored set of likely interview questions with guidance on how to answer each one well.
stages: What is the job title and industry?
What type of interview is it (behavioural, technical, panel, competency-based)?
What are the key skills or requirements listed in the job description?
What is your biggest concern going into the interview?
How much experience do you have in this type of role?

prompts.title: Write a Strong Answer to a Tough Interview Question
prompts.description: Structure a confident, clear answer to a specific interview question using a proven format.
stages: What is the interview question you need to answer?
What role or industry is this interview for?
What relevant experience or example can you draw from?
Should the answer use the STAR format (Situation, Task, Action, Result)?
How long should the answer be (30 seconds, 1 minute, 2 minutes)?

prompts.title: Negotiate a Job Offer or Salary
prompts.description: Get a clear negotiation strategy and example scripts for discussing salary, benefits, or terms.
stages: What is the offer you have received (salary, role, benefits)?
What is your target salary or what specifically do you want to negotiate?
What is your current or most recent salary?
What leverage do you have (competing offer, specialised skills, market rate)?
Should the negotiation happen over email or phone?

prompts.title: Write a Post-Interview Thank You Email
prompts.description: Send a thoughtful thank you email that reinforces your interest and leaves a strong final impression.
stages: Who did you interview with (name and title)?
What role and company was the interview for?
What was one memorable topic or moment from the interview?
What key strength or point do you want to reinforce?
How soon after the interview are you sending this?

prompts.title: Evaluate a Job Offer
prompts.description: Analyse a job offer across salary, growth, culture, and trade-offs to help you decide whether to accept.
stages: What are the full details of the offer (salary, title, benefits, location)?
What are your current compensation and working conditions?
What matters most to you in a role (salary, growth, flexibility, stability)?
Are there any concerns or red flags about the offer or company?
Do you have other offers or options you are comparing this to?

prompts.title: Create a 12-Month Career Growth Plan
prompts.description: Build a focused plan to reach your next career milestone over the next year.
stages: What is your current role and level?
What is your career goal for the next 12 months?
What skills or experience do you need to get there?
What obstacles or gaps are currently holding you back?
How much time per week can you invest in career development?

prompts.title: Identify and Close Your Skill Gaps
prompts.description: Compare your current skills to your target role and get a practical plan to close the gap.
stages: What is your current role and skill set?
What role or level are you aiming for?
What skills or qualifications does that role typically require?
Which of those skills do you already have?
What resources or learning methods work best for you?

prompts.title: Build a Case for a Promotion
prompts.description: Prepare a clear, evidence-based case to present to your manager when asking for a promotion.
stages: What is your current role and the role you are seeking?
What key achievements or contributions have you made?
How have you gone beyond your current job description?
What is the business case for promoting you now?
How do you plan to present this (email, meeting, formal review)?

prompts.title: Develop Your Professional Personal Brand
prompts.description: Define what you want to be known for professionally and how to communicate it consistently online and offline.
stages: What is your field or area of expertise?
What do you want to be known for professionally?
Who is your target audience (employers, clients, peers, industry)?
What platforms or channels will you use to build your brand?
What does your current professional presence look like?

prompts.title: Write a Difficult Workplace Email
prompts.description: Draft a professional email for a sensitive workplace situation such as pushing back, escalating an issue, or setting a boundary.
stages: What is the situation you need to address?
Who is the email going to (manager, colleague, HR, client)?
What outcome do you want from sending this email?
What tone do you need to strike (firm, diplomatic, calm, direct)?
Is there any context or history the reader needs to understand?
